commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Stage 0: Shot detection and video segmentation preprocessing

Implements shot boundary detection to split videos into continuous camera shots
before the main 3-stage pipeline runs. This ensures each segment has consistent
camera parameters, which is critical for field registration and tracking.

- ShotDetector: Multi-method fusion (frame diff + histogram) with adaptive threshold
- VideoSegmenter: Splits video at boundaries, exports frame_ranges.json
- run_stage0(): Entry point integrated into pipeline
- New --no-shot-detection flag to skip preprocessing
- Config options in default.yaml for tuning detection parameters

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign scoring system and add Investor-directed multi-run triage

Major changes to the adversarial triage skill:
- Add Socratic questioning framework (5 structured questions) for Challenger
- Support multiple Pusher-Challenger pairs (1-3 angles) with parallel execution
- Add point scoring system with asymmetric penalties per role
- Add Investor subagent for ROI-based run allocation with charter constraints
- Style-dependent scoring tables for Arbiter and Investor
- Style-dependent stopping thresholds (wide-funnel: 10%, measured: 20%, paranoid: 30%)
- Remove safe harbor: DISPUTED earns 0 (was +1) for both Pusher and Challenger
- Remove unverifiable penalties: divestment bonus and premature stop
- Explicit parallel execution order (Pushers -> Challengers -> Arbiter)
- Improved description with trigger phrases for better routing
- Effort auto-calibration from query scope/stakes/specificity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Minecraft MCP サーバーをマルチセッション対応に変更

MCP SDK の Protocol.connect() は 1 インスタンスにつき 1 トランスポートしか
受け付けないため、2 番目以降の Guild セッションで接続エラーが発生していた。
SDK 公式パターンに従い、セッションごとに新しい McpServer インスタンスを
生成するファクトリ関数方式に変更。

commit.message:
fix(entity): preserve XP/level across all unbind/rebind paths

All 7 createDefaultEntity() call sites that reset entity state now
preserve existing xp and level values. Previously only `name` was
preserved, causing XP to silently reset to 0 whenever an entity was
unbound (bot-initiated, device-owner, scheduler cleanup, official
borrow) or rebound (bind-free, bind-personal).

Fixes #156: Channel entity XP anomalous reset to 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: project x-ray — cleanup, performance, structured output, smart routing

Full-spectrum audit + upgrades from automated X-ray:

Cleanup:
- Remove dead ESLint deps + config (replaced by oxlint)
- Remove scaffold greet() command and deprecated PROVIDER_MODELS shim
- Delete empty dd-ui/dd-typewriter stub crates (0 code, 0 tests)
- Extract pipeline timeouts/retries, prompt healer thresholds to named constants
- Extract shared withTimeout utility (was duplicated in base.ts + dag.ts)
- Move Grimoire URL to VITE_GRIMOIRE_URL env var with fallback

Performance:
- Canvas: disable alpha channel (~2x compositing speedup)
- Canvas: cache ctx + font/baseline (avoid per-frame re-init)
- Canvas: color-batched draw passes (reduce fillStyle thrashing)
- Canvas: textRendering=optimizeSpeed (skip kerning/ligatures)
- SQLite: explicit WAL mode + NORMAL sync + foreign_keys=ON

Reliability:
- Provider-native structured output (Gemini responseSchema, Claude
  tool-use, OpenAI json_schema strict mode)
- JSON schemas for 5 output types (mechanics, character, backgrounds,
  options) — no new dependencies
- Existing 3-layer validation retained as safety net

Intelligence:
- Cost-aware MAB model filtering (flash vs pro tier per callsite)
- Capability-based filtering (structured output requirement)
- 20 new router tests for filtering logic

198 tests pass (178 → 198). Net -3,188 lines.

commit.message:
Guard against zero-atom molecules in smiles_to_3d_sdf

SELFIES can occasionally decode to empty/invalid SMILES that RDKit
parses as a molecule with 0 atoms, causing ValueError in EmbedMolecule.
Add GetNumAtoms() == 0 check before and after AddHs.

fix(Matching): 五项反编译细节对齐 — Newton 插值 + stride subsampling + Eigen 阈值 + scale 输出 + auto edge

- Eigen 位移阈值 0.5→0.6 对齐 dword_1800D6A8C (0x3F19999A)
- 输出 scale 移除 clamp（反编译 sub_180040150 无 clamp）
- BuildScoreGridFindPeak stride 循环对齐
- auto mode edge threshold: high=1.0, low=2.0（常量表 dword_1800D6AA8/B38 确认）

feat: adoption lifecycle — plan before adopting, nag until decided

Adoptions now follow a lifecycle:
  evaluate → plan → pending-approval → active → review → improve/ditch

Key changes:
- "Adopt" recommendation triggers a planning phase, not immediate action
- Adoption plans go to tracking/adoption-plans/[id].md with time
  estimates, risks, hypotheses with confidence levels, and rollback
- Items in pending-approval are re-surfaced every review cycle
- Items pending >7 days get escalated in digests
- Items pending >14 days trigger a direct approve/reject/defer prompt
- Pending items never silently disappear

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Force gnina to CPU to avoid VRAM conflict with LLM during GRPO

Each torchrun rank occupies one B200 GPU for the LLM. Without this fix,
gnina subprocesses would also grab GPU memory, causing OOM. Setting
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" in the subprocess env forces CPU-only docking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: tighten stale peak detection threshold from 2× to 1.5×

The previous 2× guard missed peaks set during mock-portfolio runs that
were only 1.89× the real wallet value (e.g. $291 mock peak vs $154 real
portfolio), causing a permanent drawdown halt on a fake baseline.

Any stored peak > 1.5× the live value is now treated as stale and reset
to current. A genuine 50%+ drawdown already exceeds every mandate's
maxDrawdownPct, so resetting at that point is the correct behaviour.
